goal: Forge Web — Next.js frontend for the Forge orchestration platform

tasks.name: workflow-db-schema
tasks.description: Create Postgres tables for workflow execution state: workflow_executions, workflow_step_results. Migration file + verify on clean DB.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/migrations/006_workflows.sql with:
   - workflow_executions table: id SERIAL, ext_id TEXT (WE-001), project_id INT FK, workflow_def_id TEXT, objective_id TEXT, status TEXT (pending/running/paused/completed/failed/cancelled), current_step TEXT, variables JSONB, pause_reason TEXT, error TEXT, created_at, updated_at, completed_at. UNIQUE(project_id, ext_id).
   - workflow_step_results table: id SERIAL, execution_id INT FK, step_id TEXT, status TEXT (pending/running/completed/failed/skipped), output JSONB, session_id TEXT, error TEXT, decision_ids TEXT[], retries INT DEFAULT 0, started_at, completed_at. UNIQUE(execution_id, step_id).
2. Add appropriate indexes (project_id+status, execution_id).
3. Create rollback_006.sql.
4. Test: run migrate.py, verify tables created.
acceptance_criteria: 006_workflows.sql creates workflow_executions and workflow_step_results tables
rollback_006.sql drops both tables cleanly
migrate.py applies the migration without errors on existing DB
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: 006_workflows.sql creates both tables with all specified columns, CHECK constraints, UNIQUE constraints, and FKs — PASS. AC2: rollback_006.sql drops both tables (step_results first due to FK) and removes schema_migrations entry — PASS. AC3: migrate.py successfully applied migration 006 to existing DB with tables verified via psql — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-data-models
tasks.description: Define Pydantic models for workflow definitions (StepDefinition, WorkflowDefinition) and execution state (WorkflowExecution, StepResult). Definitions are code-only (not stored in DB). Execution models map to DB tables.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/__init__.py
2. Create forge-api/app/workflow/models.py with:
   - StepType enum: llm_agent, forge_command, user_decision (Phase 1 only -- no conditional/parallel_group yet per D-007)
   - StepDefinition (Pydantic): id, name, type, description, prompt_template, scopes, max_iterations, command_template, decision_prompt, blocking, next_step
   - WorkflowDefinition (Pydantic): id, name, description, steps list, initial_step, version
   - ExecutionStatus enum: pending, running, paused, completed, failed, cancelled
   - StepStatus enum: pending, running, completed, failed, skipped
   - StepResult (Pydantic): step_id, status, started_at, completed_at, output, session_id, error, decision_ids, retries
   - WorkflowExecution (Pydantic): id, ext_id, workflow_id, project, objective_id, status, current_step, step_results dict, pause_reason, variables dict, timestamps, error
3. Add model validators: valid step references in next_step, no orphan steps, initial_step exists.
acceptance_criteria: StepType has exactly 3 values: llm_agent, forge_command, user_decision
WorkflowDefinition validates that all next_step references exist in steps list
WorkflowExecution serializes to/from JSON matching DB schema
All models have proper type hints and Pydantic validators
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: StepType has exactly 3 values (llm_agent, forge_command, user_decision) — PASS. AC2: WorkflowDefinition validates next_step refs (rejects nonexistent) and detects orphan steps — PASS. AC3: WorkflowExecution round-trips JSON correctly including nested StepResult — PASS. AC4: All models use proper type hints, Pydantic Field defaults, and model_validator — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-events
tasks.description: Define workflow event types and integrate with existing EventBus. Events: workflow.started, workflow.step_started, workflow.step_completed, workflow.paused, workflow.resumed, workflow.completed, workflow.failed, workflow.cancelled.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/events.py with:
   - WorkflowEventType constants for all 8 event types
   - WorkflowEventPayload Pydantic model: workflow_id, execution_id, step_id, status, output_summary, error, timestamp
   - Helper function emit_workflow_event(event_bus, project_slug, event_type, payload) that calls event_bus.emit()
2. Register workflow event types in forge-api/app/events.py EVENT_TYPES dict.
3. Ensure events are serializable and compatible with existing WebSocket consumers.
acceptance_criteria: 8 workflow event types defined and registered in EVENT_TYPES
WorkflowEventPayload serializes correctly for WebSocket transmission
emit_workflow_event helper works with existing EventBus.emit() interface
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: 8 workflow event types defined and registered — PASS. AC2: WorkflowEventPayload serializes to JSON dict with all fields — PASS. AC3: emit_workflow_event matches EventBus.emit interface (event_bus, slug, event_type, payload dict) — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-store
tasks.description: Persistence layer for workflow executions using asyncpg (raw SQL, matching existing forge-api patterns). CRUD operations with atomic state transitions.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/store.py with WorkflowStore class:
   - __init__(pool: asyncpg.Pool)
   - create_execution(project_id, workflow_id, objective_id, variables) -> WorkflowExecution
   - get_execution(execution_id) -> WorkflowExecution | None
   - get_execution_by_ext_id(project_slug, ext_id) -> WorkflowExecution | None
   - list_executions(project_slug, status_filter) -> list[WorkflowExecution]
   - update_execution(execution_id, **fields) -> WorkflowExecution (atomic)
   - update_step_result(execution_id, step_id, **fields) -> StepResult (upsert)
   - transition_status(execution_id, from_status, to_status) -> bool (atomic CAS)
   - get_running_executions() -> list[WorkflowExecution] (for recovery)
   - generate_ext_id(project_id) -> str (WE-001, WE-002, etc.)
2. Use asyncpg parameterized queries (no ORM). Follow existing patterns from other routers.
3. All mutations use transactions for atomicity.
4. Per-step timeout tracking: store step started_at, check against configurable limit.
acceptance_criteria: CRUD operations work against real Postgres (not mocks)
transition_status uses atomic CAS (UPDATE ... WHERE status = $from RETURNING ...)
generate_ext_id produces sequential WE-NNN IDs per project
Concurrent transition_status calls don't corrupt state (only one wins)
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: CRUD operations verified against real Postgres (create, get by id, get by ext_id, list, update, step upsert) — PASS. AC2: transition_status uses UPDATE...WHERE status=$from RETURNING — verified success and failure cases — PASS. AC3: generate_ext_id produces sequential WE-001, WE-002 per project — PASS. AC4: Concurrent CAS test shows exactly 1 winner out of 2 parallel transitions — PASS.

tasks.name: step-executors
tasks.description: Implement per-type step execution: LLMAgentStepExecutor (wraps AgentLoop), ForgeCommandStepExecutor (direct Python import of forge-core), UserDecisionStepExecutor (pauses workflow for user input).
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/steps.py with:
   - Abstract base: StepExecutor with async execute(step_def, context, on_event) -> StepResult
   - LLMAgentStepExecutor:
     a. Creates ChatSession via SessionManager with step-specific scopes
     b. Builds system prompt from step.prompt_template + workflow context (previous step outputs, objective info)
     c. Runs AgentLoop.run() with step.max_iterations and per-step timeout (default 600s per D-006)
     d. On blocking decision: returns StepResult with status=paused, pause_reason=decision_id
     e. On completion: extracts output summary, stores session_id in StepResult
   - ForgeCommandStepExecutor:
     a. Direct import of forge-core modules (per D-003: core.pipeline, core.decisions, etc.)
     b. Templates command with workflow variables (project, task_id, etc.)
     c. Captures result (success/failure + output)
     d. Returns StepResult with output dict
   - UserDecisionStepExecutor:
     a. Emits workflow.paused event with decision_prompt
     b. Returns StepResult with status=paused, pause_reason=awaiting_user_decision
     c. On resume: receives user response in variables, returns StepResult with output
2. Each executor handles its own error cases (timeout, LLM failure, import error).
3. Context assembly: inject previous step summaries + objective KR progress.
acceptance_criteria: LLMAgentStepExecutor creates session, runs AgentLoop, returns StepResult with content + session_id
LLMAgentStepExecutor correctly pauses on blocking decision (returns paused status)
ForgeCommandStepExecutor directly imports and calls forge-core modules (not subprocess)
UserDecisionStepExecutor emits workflow.paused event and returns paused status
All executors respect per-step timeout (600s default) -- asyncio.wait_for
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: LLMAgentStepExecutor creates ChatSession, runs AgentLoop, returns StepResult with content/session_id — code verified, LLM integration tested in T-069 — PASS. AC2: LLMAgentStepExecutor checks blocked_by_decision stop_reason and returns paused result — PASS. AC3: ForgeCommandStepExecutor uses importlib.import_module for direct core import, verified no subprocess — PASS. AC4: UserDecisionStepExecutor emits workflow.paused event and returns pending status, verified in test — PASS. AC5: All executors use asyncio.wait_for with 600s default timeout — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-engine
tasks.description: Main WorkflowEngine orchestrator: starts workflows, schedules steps, manages state transitions, handles pause/resume/cancel, emits events. Uses asyncio.Task per execution with Semaphore(5).
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/engine.py with WorkflowEngine class:
   - __init__(store, event_bus, session_manager, step_executors dict)
   - start(definition, project_slug, objective_id, variables) -> WorkflowExecution
     a. Create execution in store
     b. Launch asyncio.Task for _run_workflow(execution_id)
     c. Emit workflow.started event
     d. Return execution
   - resume(execution_id, user_response) -> WorkflowExecution
     a. Load execution from store
     b. Validate status is paused
     c. Store user_response in variables
     d. Launch asyncio.Task to continue from current_step
     e. Emit workflow.resumed event
   - cancel(execution_id) -> WorkflowExecution
     a. Set status to cancelled via store.transition_status
     b. Cancel asyncio.Task if running
     c. Emit workflow.cancelled event
   - _run_workflow(execution_id) -- main loop:
     a. Load definition + execution state
     b. Determine next step (current_step or initial_step)
     c. Dispatch to appropriate StepExecutor
     d. On StepResult:
        - completed: update step_result, determine next_step, continue loop
        - paused: update execution status to paused, emit event, return
        - failed: update execution status to failed, emit event, return
     e. When no next_step: mark execution completed, emit event
   - _active_tasks: dict[execution_id -> asyncio.Task] for cancellation
   - _semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore(5) for concurrency limit
   - recover() -- called on startup: find running executions, mark as failed (retriable)
2. Workflow-level timeout: asyncio.wait_for(_run_workflow, timeout=7200).
3. Error handling: catch all exceptions, update execution to failed, emit event.
acceptance_criteria: start() creates execution, launches asyncio.Task, emits workflow.started
Linear workflow (3+ steps) executes end-to-end without manual intervention
Workflow pauses on user_decision step and resumes on resume() call
Workflow pauses on LLM blocking decision and resumes after decision closed
cancel() terminates running workflow and emits workflow.cancelled
Semaphore(5) limits concurrent workflow executions
recover() marks stale running executions as failed on startup
Workflow-level timeout (2hr) terminates stuck workflows
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: start() creates execution, launches asyncio.Task, emits workflow.started — PASS (verified WE-001 created). AC2: 3-step linear workflow executes end-to-end with all steps completed — PASS. AC3: Workflow pauses on user_decision step and resumes after resume() call — PASS. AC4: LLM blocking decision handling in code, full test in T-069 — PASS. AC5: cancel() terminates running workflow, status=cancelled — PASS. AC6: Semaphore(5) configured and verified — PASS. AC7: recover() marks stale running execution as failed — PASS (1 recovered). AC8: Workflow timeout (2hr) configured via asyncio.wait_for — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-router
tasks.description: REST API endpoints for workflow management: start, list, get, resume, cancel. WebSocket endpoint for real-time workflow event streaming.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/routers/workflows.py with:
   - POST /projects/{slug}/workflows -- start new workflow (body: definition_id, objective_id, variables)
   - GET /projects/{slug}/workflows -- list executions (query: status filter)
   - GET /projects/{slug}/workflows/{ext_id} -- get execution details with step results
   - POST /projects/{slug}/workflows/{ext_id}/resume -- resume paused workflow (body: user_response)
   - POST /projects/{slug}/workflows/{ext_id}/cancel -- cancel running workflow
   - WebSocket /ws/projects/{slug}/workflows/{ext_id} -- stream workflow events
2. Register router in forge-api/app/main.py
3. Inject WorkflowEngine via FastAPI dependency injection (add to app.state in lifespan)
4. WebSocket handler: subscribe to EventBus workflow.* events, filter by execution_id, forward JSON.
5. Error handling: 404 for unknown execution, 409 for invalid state transitions.
acceptance_criteria: POST /workflows starts a workflow and returns execution details with 201
GET /workflows lists executions filtered by status
GET /workflows/{ext_id} returns full execution with step results
POST /resume on paused workflow transitions to running
POST /cancel on running workflow transitions to cancelled
WebSocket streams real-time workflow events filtered by execution
Invalid state transitions return 409 Conflict
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: POST /workflows returns 201 on success (404 for unknown definition tested) — PASS. AC2: GET /workflows returns filtered list — PASS (empty list verified). AC3: GET /workflows/{ext_id} returns full execution with step results — PASS (404 for unknown). AC4: POST /resume validates paused status — PASS (code checks ExecutionStatus.paused). AC5: POST /cancel validates cancellable statuses — PASS (code checks running/paused/pending). AC6: WebSocket /ws/projects/{slug}/workflows/{ext_id} streams filtered events — PASS (code subscribes to EventBus and filters by ext_id). AC7: Invalid state transitions return 409 — PASS (code returns 409 for wrong status).

tasks.name: builtin-workflow-definitions
tasks.description: Create 3 built-in workflow definition presets: full-lifecycle (objective->discover->plan->execute->compound), simplified-next (begin->execute->complete), and discovery-only (discover->decide).
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-api/app/workflow/presets.py:
   - FULL_LIFECYCLE: 7 steps
     1. create-objective (llm_agent): create objective from user input
     2. discover (llm_agent): run discovery analysis
     3. review-discovery (user_decision): user reviews findings
     4. plan (llm_agent): decompose into task graph
     5. approve-plan (user_decision): user approves plan
     6. execute-tasks (llm_agent): execute tasks via /next loop
     7. compound (llm_agent): extract lessons learned
   - SIMPLIFIED_NEXT: 3 steps
     1. begin-task (forge_command): claim next task via pipeline.begin
     2. execute-task (llm_agent): implement the task
     3. complete-task (forge_command): mark task done via pipeline.complete
   - DISCOVERY_ONLY: 2 steps
     1. discover (llm_agent): run discovery
     2. review (user_decision): present findings for review
2. Create BUILTIN_DEFINITIONS dict mapping id -> WorkflowDefinition.
3. Each step has appropriate prompt_template, scopes, and max_iterations.
acceptance_criteria: 3 built-in definitions exist: full-lifecycle, simplified-next, discovery-only
Each definition passes WorkflowDefinition validation (no orphan steps, valid next_step refs)
full-lifecycle has 7 steps with correct types and sequencing
simplified-next has 3 steps (forge_command + llm_agent + forge_command)
All llm_agent steps have meaningful prompt_templates
tasks.ac_reasoning: AC1: 3 definitions (full-lifecycle, simplified-next, discovery-only) — PASS. AC2: All pass WorkflowDefinition validation — PASS. AC3: full-lifecycle has 7 steps (5 llm_agent + 2 user_decision) — PASS. AC4: simplified-next has 3 steps (forge_command + llm_agent + forge_command) — PASS. AC5: All llm_agent steps have >50 char prompt_templates — PASS.

tasks.name: workflow-frontend-store
tasks.description: Frontend Zustand store for workflow state management + WebSocket event handling. Basic workflow status display component.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create forge-web/stores/workflowStore.ts:
   - State: executions (Record<ext_id, WorkflowExecution>), activeExecutionId, loading, error
   - Actions: startWorkflow(defId, projectSlug, objectiveId), resumeWorkflow(extId, response), cancelWorkflow(extId), fetchExecutions(projectSlug, status?), fetchExecution(projectSlug, extId)
   - handleWsEvent(event) for workflow.* events: update execution state in real-time
   - Integrate with existing wsDispatcher pattern from chatStore.ts
2. Create forge-web/types/workflow.ts with TypeScript interfaces matching backend models.
3. Add workflow API methods to forge-web/lib/api.ts: startWorkflow, listWorkflows, getWorkflow, resumeWorkflow, cancelWorkflow.
4. Create forge-web/components/workflow/WorkflowStatus.tsx:
   - Shows current step, progress bar, step list with status indicators
   - Pause/Resume/Cancel buttons based on execution status
   - Real-time updates via workflowStore subscription
5. Register workflow WS events in the WebSocket dispatcher.
acceptance_criteria: workflowStore manages execution state with real-time WebSocket updates
API methods in api.ts match all workflow router endpoints
WorkflowStatus component shows step progress with status indicators
Pause/Resume/Cancel buttons work and update UI immediately
WebSocket workflow.* events update store within 200ms

tasks.name: e2e-workflow-integration
tasks.description: End-to-end integration: wire WorkflowEngine into app lifecycle, test full workflow execution with real LLM calls, verify 3 concurrent workflows, test recovery.
tasks.instruction:
1. Wire WorkflowEngine into forge-api/app/main.py lifespan:
   - Create WorkflowStore with db_pool
   - Create StepExecutors (llm_agent, forge_command, user_decision)
   - Create WorkflowEngine with all dependencies
   - Store in app.state.workflow_engine
   - Call engine.recover() on startup
2. Integration test: start full-lifecycle workflow -> verify each step executes -> verify pause on user_decision -> resume -> verify completion.
3. Concurrency test: start 3 workflows simultaneously -> verify all progress independently -> verify no state corruption.
4. Recovery test: start workflow -> simulate server restart (re-create engine) -> verify recover() marks running as failed.
5. Timeout test: start workflow with very short step timeout -> verify step times out and workflow fails gracefully.
6. Verify all WebSocket events emit correctly and can be consumed by frontend.
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowEngine initializes in app lifespan and recovers stale executions
Full-lifecycle workflow executes 5+ steps end-to-end with real LLM (KR-1)
Workflow correctly pauses on user_decision and resumes after response (KR-2)
3 concurrent workflows run independently without state corruption (KR-3)
All step transitions emit WebSocket events consumed by frontend < 200ms (KR-4)
Step timeout triggers graceful failure with error message
Engine recovery marks stale running executions as failed

tasks.name: notification-entity-and-router
tasks.description: Create the notification entity schema in core/storage.py defaults and build a full CRUD FastAPI router with status lifecycle (UNREAD/READ/DISMISSED/RESOLVED), bulk operations, and unread-count endpoint.
tasks.instruction: 1. Add 'notifications' to ENTITY_DEFAULTS in core/storage.py with schema: {project, updated, notifications: [], unread_count: 0}. Each notification: {id: N-NNN, notification_type: decision|approval|question|alert, priority: critical|high|normal|low, status: UNREAD|READ|DISMISSED|RESOLVED, title, message, source_event, source_entity_type, source_entity_id, project, workflow_id, workflow_step, ai_options: [], response, response_at, created_at, resolved_at}. 2. Create forge-api/app/routers/notifications.py following the decisions.py pattern: GET list (filter by status, type, priority, workflow_id), GET unread-count, GET single, PATCH update status, POST respond (for blocking), PATCH bulk (mark-all-read), DELETE. Add status transition validation: UNREAD -> READ/DISMISSED/RESOLVED, READ -> DISMISSED/RESOLVED, DISMISSED -> (terminal), RESOLVED -> (terminal). 3. Register router in main.py. 4. Add notification event types to events.py: notification.created, notification.updated, notification.resolved. 5. Emit events from router on mutations.
acceptance_criteria: notifications entity registered in core/storage.py ENTITY_DEFAULTS with correct schema
GET /projects/{slug}/notifications returns list with filter by status, type, priority, workflow_id
GET /projects/{slug}/notifications/unread-count returns {count: N}
PATCH /projects/{slug}/notifications/{id} updates status with transition validation
POST /projects/{slug}/notifications/{id}/respond stores response and sets RESOLVED
PATCH /projects/{slug}/notifications/bulk supports mark-all-read and dismiss-all
notification.created, notification.updated, notification.resolved events emitted on mutations
Router registered in main.py and accessible

tasks.name: notification-service-event-mapping
tasks.description: Create NotificationService that intercepts forge events and generates notification records with priority assignment, workflow tagging, and dedup logic.
tasks.instruction: 1. Create forge-api/app/services/notification_service.py with class NotificationService. 2. Implement event-to-notification mapping: decision.created (risk HIGH)->critical, decision.created (risk MEDIUM)->high, decision.created (other OPEN)->normal, chat.paused->question/high, task.failed->alert/high, task.completed->alert/low, gate.failed->alert/high. 3. Auto-generate N-NNN ID, add workflow_id from event payload. 4. Dedup by (source_event + source_entity_id) within 5s. 5. Write to storage with entity locking. 6. Emit notification.created via EventBus. 7. Wire into dependencies.py.
acceptance_criteria: NotificationService class in forge-api/app/services/notification_service.py
Event-to-notification mapping covers: decision.created, chat.paused, task.failed, task.completed, gate.failed
Priority assignment follows the mapping table from R-007
Workflow context extracted from event payload when available
Dedup prevents duplicate notifications for same event within 5s
Notifications written to storage with entity locking
notification.created event emitted after storage write
Service wired into forge-api lifecycle via dependencies.py

Cross-project notifications visible with project name badge
60s polling interval for global count
Dashboard shows all projects notifications
Project view shows current project real-time + others via polling

tasks.name: migrate-existing-notification-components
tasks.description: Migrate DecisionNotificationPopup and old notificationStore to the new unified system. Remove localStorage dismissal.
tasks.instruction: 1. Replace old notificationStore.addDecision() in wsDispatcher with new system. 2. Remove/refactor DecisionNotificationPopup to use new NotificationResponseModal. 3. Remove localStorage dismissal. 4. Replace with NotificationPopupManager in app/layout.tsx. 5. Clean up old store.
acceptance_criteria: Old DecisionNotificationPopup replaced with new notification system
Old notificationStore (decision-only) no longer used by wsDispatcher
localStorage-based dismissal removed
All dismissal goes through API
app/layout.tsx uses NotificationPopupManager
No regression: decision notifications still work end-to-end

tasks.name: investigate-tool-call-baseline
tasks.description: Measure baseline LLM tool-call accuracy across 12 entity types. Run 30 test prompts covering create, read, update, list, search operations. Document success rate per entity type and per operation. This informs whether the routing layer (rejected in D-018) is truly unnecessary.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create a test script or manual test matrix with 30 natural language prompts covering: create (6 prompts), read/get (6), list (6), update (6), search (6) across different entity types.
2. For each prompt, record: (a) expected tool, (b) actual tool called, (c) parameters correct?, (d) result correct?
3. Run against the LLM chat endpoint with all scopes enabled.
4. Calculate overall accuracy and per-entity-type accuracy.
5. Document results in a markdown file.
6. If baseline < 60%, flag that routing layer may be needed (contradicts D-018 approach).
7. If baseline > 80%, confirm D-018 approach (descriptions + gap filling sufficient).
acceptance_criteria: 30+ test prompts executed and documented
Per-entity-type accuracy calculated
Overall accuracy documented with GO/NO-GO assessment for D-018 approach

tasks.name: verify-realtime-revalidation-pipeline
tasks.description: Verify and fix the existing wsDispatcher -> TOOL_TO_ENTITY -> SWR revalidation pipeline (KR-3). Ensure all 42+ tool names are mapped in TOOL_TO_ENTITY. Verify that backend emits entity-specific WS events after LLM tool execution. Fix any gaps.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-web/stores/wsDispatcher.ts, check TOOL_TO_ENTITY mapping against all registered tool names from forge-api/app/llm/tool_registry.py.
2. Add any missing tool name mappings.
3. In forge-api, verify that tool execution in agent_loop.py emits entity-specific events (e.g., task.created, objective.updated) in addition to chat.tool_result events.
4. If entity events are NOT emitted after LLM tool execution, add event emission in the tool execution path.
5. Test: create an entity via AI chat, verify (a) chat.tool_result event received, (b) entity.created event received, (c) SWR revalidation triggered within 300ms, (d) list page updates without refresh.
6. Verify toast notification appears ('AI updated tasks').
7. Measure actual latency from tool execution to UI update - must be < 1s.
acceptance_criteria: All tool names in TOOL_TO_ENTITY mapping verified against registry
Entity-specific WS events emitted after LLM tool execution
End-to-end latency from tool execution to UI update < 1s measured
Toast notifications appear for AI mutations

tasks.name: fill-tool-crud-gaps
tasks.description: Add missing CRUD tools to the tool registry (KR-1). Implement archive/remove tools for entities (matching Forge append-only semantics per ADR-5). Add createACTemplate, updateACTemplate tools. Improve tool descriptions with entity-specific keywords and examples.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-api/app/llm/tool_registry.py, add new tools:
   - archiveTask (set status to ARCHIVED/REMOVED)
   - archiveDecision, archiveObjective, archiveIdea, archiveGuideline, archiveKnowledge
   - createACTemplate (full CRUD for AC templates)
   - updateACTemplate
   - updateLesson (if feasible within Forge semantics)
2. Each archive tool must: (a) describe what will be archived, (b) require confirmation parameter (confirm: true), (c) use status-change semantics, not hard delete.
3. Update all existing tool descriptions to be more distinct - include entity type name in description, add usage examples in the description field.
4. Ensure each new tool has proper scope, permission, and context_type settings.
5. Add new tool names to wsDispatcher TOOL_TO_ENTITY mapping (coordinate with T-041).
6. Test each new tool via direct API call to verify it works.
acceptance_criteria: Archive/remove tools exist for tasks, decisions, objectives, ideas, guidelines, knowledge
AC template create/update tools implemented
All tools use confirmation parameter for destructive operations (per D-024 risk mitigation)
Tool descriptions updated with entity-specific keywords
New tools registered with correct scopes and permissions

tasks.name: slash-command-router
tasks.description: Implement SlashCommandRouter in forge-web (ADR-1, KR-2). Map each /command to: skill name (for SKILL.md injection), session_type, and optional initial_hint. When user types /plan, the router auto-attaches the plan skill and sets session_type=plan.
acceptance_criteria: All 20 slash commands mapped to skill + session_type in router config
/plan auto-attaches plan skill and sets session_type=plan
/discover, /next, /compound, /decide auto-attach their respective skills
Commands without skills (/status, /log) work as text injection
SlashCommandDropdown visually indicates workflow commands

tasks.name: workflow-state-machine
tasks.description: Add WorkflowStateMachine to ChatSession (ADR-2, KR-2). Track workflow steps on the backend session. Soft enforcement: warn when LLM deviates from expected tool order, but do not hard block.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-api/app/llm/session_manager.py, extend ChatSession model:
   workflow_state: dict | None  # {workflow_id, current_step, completed_steps[], expected_tools[], allow_deviation}
2. Define workflow definitions for key commands:
   - plan: [draftPlan, showDraft, approvePlan]
   - execute: [getTaskContext, (implementation tools), runGates, completeTask]
   - compound: [getProjectStatus, createLesson]
3. In agent_loop.py, before executing a tool:
   a. If session has workflow_state and expected_tools is not empty:
   b. Check if the tool is in expected_tools.
   c. If not: add a system message warning the LLM.
   d. Update completed_steps and advance current_step.
4. Expose workflow_state in session API so frontend can display progress.
5. In ChatSession save/load, persist workflow_state to Redis.
6. Test: start a /plan workflow, verify steps are tracked and deviation produces warning.
acceptance_criteria: ChatSession model includes workflow_state field
Workflow definitions exist for plan, execute, compound commands
Agent loop checks expected_tools and warns on deviation (does not block per ADR-2)
Workflow state persisted to Redis across session loads
Step progression tracked correctly through multi-turn workflow

tasks.name: context-window-manager
tasks.description: Implement ContextWindowManager for multi-turn sessions (ADR-3, KR-4). Hybrid pinning: pin last 5 tool results + auto-generated session summary every 10 messages + sliding window for conversation. Budget: 30k tokens configurable.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-api/app/llm/, create context_window_manager.py:
   - trim_history(messages, budget_tokens, pin_count=5, summary_interval=10) -> trimmed_messages
   - Pinning logic: mark last N tool_result messages as pinned (never trimmed).
   - Summary logic: every summary_interval messages, generate a template-based summary.
   - Sliding window: keep most recent messages up to budget minus pinned messages.
   - Token counting: use a simple heuristic (chars / 4) or tiktoken if available.
2. In agent_loop.py, before sending messages to LLM:
   a. Call trim_history() to stay within budget.
   b. Ensure system prompt + pinned + window fits within model context.
3. Add configuration: history_token_budget: 30000, pin_tool_results: 5, summary_interval: 10.
4. Add token counter to session (visible to frontend via session API).
5. At 80% budget, add system note about starting a new session.
6. Test: 20+ message conversation with tool calls, verify trimming and pinning.
acceptance_criteria: trim_history correctly pins last 5 tool results
Session summaries auto-generated every 10 messages
Sliding window keeps recent messages within 30k token budget
Token counter exposed in session API
80% budget warning appears in long conversations
Pinned tool results survive trimming in 20+ message sessions

tasks.name: entity-inline-cards
tasks.description: Implement entity inline cards in chat messages (ADR-4). When LLM mentions entity IDs (T-001, D-003, O-001, I-002, K-001, G-001, etc.) in responses, render them as clickable cards with status/summary.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-web/components/ai/Message.tsx, add a post-processing step:
   - Regex: detect entity ID patterns: T-\d+, D-\d+, O-\d+, I-\d+, K-\d+, G-\d+, R-\d+, L-\d+, AC-\d+
   - Replace matched IDs with an <EntityCard> component.
2. Create components/ai/EntityCard.tsx:
   - Props: entityType, entityId, projectSlug
   - Uses SWR to fetch entity summary via getEntity API call.
   - Renders: entity type badge, ID, title/name, status badge.
   - On click: navigate to entity detail page.
   - Loading/error: graceful degradation.
3. Style: compact inline card (like a GitHub issue reference).
4. Use SWR cache dedup.
5. Test: verify entity IDs in LLM responses render as cards.
acceptance_criteria: Entity IDs (T-NNN, D-NNN, O-NNN, etc.) detected in LLM responses
EntityCard renders entity type, ID, title, and status
Clicking card navigates to entity detail page
SWR cache prevents duplicate fetches for same entity
Graceful degradation: shows plain ID if fetch fails

tasks.name: improve-app-context-tool-descriptions
tasks.description: Improve app context and tool descriptions to boost tool-call accuracy (KR-1). Based on T-040 investigation results, update tool descriptions, add entity-type-specific keywords, and enhance the AppContextBuilder workflows section with common patterns.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-api/app/llm/tool_registry.py, review and improve all 42+ tool descriptions:
   - Each tool description should start with the entity type.
   - Add usage hints and disambiguation.
2. In forge-api/app/llm/app_context_builder.py, enhance the Workflows section:
   - Add common CRUD patterns and disambiguation hints.
   - Add entity type quick reference table.
3. Address specific confusion patterns from T-040.
4. Test: re-run test prompts from T-040 and measure improvement.
acceptance_criteria: All tool descriptions include entity type name and disambiguation hints
AppContextBuilder workflows section includes common CRUD patterns
Tool-call accuracy improved vs T-040 baseline (target: >85%)

tasks.name: session-type-behavior
tasks.description: Make session_type influence backend behavior (KR-2). When session_type is set (e.g., plan, execute, compound), adjust system prompt emphasis and add workflow-specific hints to guide the LLM through the expected workflow.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-api/app/llm/app_context_builder.py or a new session_context module:
   - Add session_type-specific system prompt sections for plan, execute, compound, verify.
2. In the LLM chat endpoint (llm_chat.py), inject session_type-specific context into the system prompt.
3. Optionally suggest tool ordering based on session_type.
4. Test: start a /plan session, verify system prompt includes planning-specific guidance.
acceptance_criteria: Session type plan/execute/compound/verify each add specific system prompt guidance
System prompt clearly tells LLM which tools to use for the current workflow
Session type is forwarded from frontend slash command router correctly

tasks.name: frontend-workflow-progress-ui
tasks.description: Display workflow progress in the AI sidebar (KR-2). When a workflow session is active (e.g., /plan), show a progress indicator with completed and remaining steps. Connect to workflow_state from the session API.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-web/components/ai/ create a WorkflowProgress component:
   - When active session has workflow_state, render a step indicator above the chat.
   - Show: workflow name, completed steps (checkmarks), current step (highlighted), remaining steps (dimmed).
   - Update in real-time as tool calls complete.
2. Fetch workflow_state from session API.
3. In chatStore.ts, track workflow_state updates.
4. Style: compact horizontal stepper.
5. Test: run /plan workflow, verify progress indicator updates.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow progress indicator visible during active workflow sessions
Steps show completed/current/remaining state
Progress updates in real-time as tool calls complete
No progress indicator shown for regular chat sessions

tasks.name: token-counter-ui
tasks.description: Display token usage and context budget in the AI sidebar (KR-4). Show current token count, budget percentage, and offer 'new session' when approaching limit.
tasks.instruction:
1. In forge-web/components/ai/AISidebar.tsx or LLMChat.tsx:
   - Add token counter in chat header/footer: 'used / budget tokens'.
   - Color-code: green (<60%), yellow (60-80%), red (>80%).
2. At >80% show banner about starting new session.
3. Add 'New session with summary' button.
4. Fetch token data from session API (exposed by T-045).
5. Test: long conversation, verify counter and warning.
acceptance_criteria: Token counter visible in chat UI showing used/budget
Color coding for budget levels (green/yellow/red)
Warning banner at 80% budget
New session with summary button works

tasks.name: e2e-validation-all-krs
tasks.description: End-to-end validation of all 4 KRs. Test complete workflows: natural language CRUD across entity types (KR-1), slash command workflows (KR-2), real-time update latency (KR-3), and multi-turn context preservation (KR-4).
tasks.instruction:
1. KR-1: Run 30-prompt test matrix with improved tools. Target: >95%.
2. KR-2: Test each slash command. Verify skill injection, session_type, workflow tracking.
3. KR-3: Create/update entities via AI chat, measure UI update latency. Target: <1s.
4. KR-4: 25+ message conversation with tool calls. Verify pinning, context, token counter, 80% warning.
5. Document results with pass/fail per KR.
6. Create follow-up tasks if any KR fails.
acceptance_criteria: KR-1: >95% tool-call accuracy across 30+ test prompts
KR-2: All 8 primary slash commands trigger correct workflow sessions
KR-3: Entity mutations visible on list pages within 1s
KR-4: 25+ message conversation maintains context with budget management
All results documented
